title: Voronoi Cells — The Territory of Each Lattice Point
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: The Voronoï cells of the Eisenstein integer lattice are regular hexagons, which tile the plane with zero gaps and zero overlap
content.proof: The Voronoï cell of a lattice point is the set of all points closer to that point than any other. For the hexagonal lattice, perpendicular bisectors of lines to the 6 nearest neighbors form a regular hexagon. All hexagons are congruent. They tile the plane perfectly — no gaps, no overlaps, no distortion.
content.benchmark: Voronoi cell area: √3/2 ≈ 0.866. Square lattice cell area: 1.0. The hexagonal cell is 13.4% smaller, meaning more lattice points per unit area = denser packing.
content.caveat: In 2D, the hexagonal Voronoï tessellation is unique in its optimality. In 3D+, the Kepler conjecture (proved by Hales) shows FCC/HCP is optimal, but Voronoï cells are not regular.
